questions.question: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which is the customer's responsibility for an AI workload?

answers.text: Configuring access (IAM), and protecting the data they put into the service
Securing the physical data centres running the managed service
Patching the underlying Bedrock service infrastructure

questions.feedback: AWS secures the cloud (infrastructure); the customer secures what they put in it, data, access policies, and configuration.

questions.question: You need an audit trail of who invoked which model and when. Which service?
questions.feedback: CloudTrail records API activity (who did what). Artifact is for compliance reports; Polly is text-to-speech.

questions.question: Where do you obtain AWS compliance reports such as SOC and ISO?
questions.feedback: AWS Artifact is the self-service portal for compliance reports and agreements.

questions.question: Which statements about securing and governing AI on AWS are true?

answers.text: Amazon Macie helps discover and protect sensitive data (PII) in S3
With Bedrock, your prompts/data are not used to train the base foundation models
SageMaker Model Cards help document a model's details and intended use
Encrypting training data is unnecessary if the bucket is private

questions.feedback: Macie protects PII, Bedrock keeps your data out of base-model training, and Model Cards aid model governance. Encryption at rest is still best practice even for private buckets.
